People: 1415: John Murphy was marked on the right shoulder with the letter M, one inch long; the letter being made by pricking in India ink. A six inch hollow shot was suspended to his waist by a chain, and he was confined in the brig.

Noon: There is a long note in red concerning the weather data. Generally accurate but wind force is generally much underestimated.

Other: 1800: During the day the water has been a deep blue and at dark noticed millions of Pyrosomas from 2 to 5 in long. The water completely filled with them and the wake very brilliant. I have never noticed them before in this locality.

2000: The wake very brilliant with phosphorescent light. Several patches and streaks of brilliantly white water suddenly appeared near the Ship and on passing through one of them, it seemed to be only the light from numerous Pyrosomas mollusces. These all disappeared by 10 o'clock.

Note: 1400: The sea temperature of 84 was crossed out by a later editor. I left it in.
0400: Owing to the loss of both log chips and a portion of the lines in the 1st watch, was obliged to log the Ship according to the best of my judgment.
The pressures from here to 1861-09-27 1800 are about 0.20 inches too low.
